entities.name: SOIL MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Practices used to manage soil to affect runoff and infiltration processes

entities.name: RUNOFF
entities.description: The flow of water that occurs when excess stormwater runs off the land surface

entities.name: INFILTRATION
entities.description: The process by which water on the ground surface enters the soil

entities.name: ALFISOL
entities.description: A type of soil characterized as hardsetting and structurally unstable in semi-arid tropics

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographic region characterized by specific climatic conditions

entities.name: STRUCTURAL INSTABILITY
entities.description: The characteristic of Alfisols that makes them prone to surface sealing under raindrop impact

entities.name: RAINDROPS
entities.description: The physical impact of falling rain on the soil surface

entities.name: SURFACE SEAL
entities.description: A thin layer on the soil surface that reduces infiltration and increases runoff

entities.name: PLANT-AVAILABLE WATER
entities.description: The portion of soil water that can be absorbed by plant roots

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area
